title: Versant Media (VSNT) Acquires StockStory to Boost CNBC Digital Platforms
content:
Versant Media Group Inc. (NASDAQ:VSNT) is one of the

10 Best Young Stocks to Buy Right Now. Versant Media Group Inc. (NASDAQ:VSNT) is one of the best young stocks to buy right now. On April 2, Versant Media Group announced the acquisition of StockStory, which is an AI-powered financial analysis platform. This move is designed to bolster CNBC’s digital capabilities by integrating technology that provides real-time, actionable market insights and stock recommendations.

The acquisition aligns with Versant’s broader strategy to expand its core brands into new digital platforms and services. The integration of StockStory’s ML and data-driven frameworks will allow CNBC to provide faster and more scalable analysis of public companies. Deep Bagchee, Chief Product & Technology Officer for News at Versant, noted that the addition of these capabilities is intended to deepen engagement among retail investors across CNBC’s digital portfolio.

As part of the agreement, StockStory Founder and CEO Adam Hejl will join Versant Media Group Inc. (NASDAQ:VSNT), reporting to Deep Bagchee. The StockStory team will transition to support various product and technology initiatives, focusing initially on enhancing the digital investing tools available through CNBC’s platforms.Versant Media (VSNT) Acquires StockStory to Boost CNBC Digital Platforms

Photo by Memento Media on UnsplashVersant Media (VSNT) Acquires StockStory to Boost CNBC Digital Platforms

Photo by Memento Media on Unsplash

Versant Media Group Inc. (NASDAQ:VSNT) is a media company that produces, licenses, and acquires news, sports, and entertainment content. Versant Media was formerly a subsidiary of Comcast Corporation.

While we acknowledge the potential of VSNT as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. title: Versant Media Acquires AI Firm StockStory to Boost CNBC Digital Strategy
content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Versant Media Group (NASDAQ:VSNT) is leaning further into AI as it looks to reshape how financial content is delivered, announcing the acquisition of StockStory, an AI-powered investment research platform, to support CNBC's digital expansion. The company, which was spun out of Comcast (NASDAQ:CMCSA) and houses CNBC and MS NOW, did not disclose financial terms, but management indicated the deal is intended to strengthen its ability to deliver faster, data-driven insights for investors operating in increasingly real-time markets.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 4 Warning Sign with VSNT. Is VSNT fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

StockStory's platform focuses on analyzing public companies by combining data, machine learning, AI, and editorial frameworks to generate financial analysis, market insights, and stock recommendations. Versant said the technology could enhance CNBC's ability to produce more actionable analysis at greater speed, potentially improving how investors interpret market developments as they unfold. As part of the transaction, StockStory founder and CEO Adam Hejl will join Versant and report to Deep Bagchee, Chief Product and Technology Officer for News, a structure that could signal deeper integration between product development and newsroom execution.

The acquisition also aligns with Versant's broader deal-making strategy since its Nasdaq debut earlier this year, where it has already acquired Free TV Networks, a provider of over-the-air digital broadcasts, and INDY Cinema Group, a technology platform for movie theaters. A March report from The New York Times indicated the company is also weighing a potential acquisition of Vox Media's podcast network, suggesting Versant could be building a wider ecosystem that blends distribution scale with data-driven content capabilities across platforms.

title: Versant acquires AI-driven financial insights platform StockStory
content:
Versant Media Group (VSNT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VSNT]), the networks-focused entity that spun out of Comcast (CMCSA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMCSA]) and the parent company of CNBC and MS NOW, said it has acquired AI-powered StockStory to support growth for CNBC’s digital platform.

Financial terms of the deal were not disclosed.

StockStory delivers financial analysis, market insights, and stock recommendations. Its technology focuses on analysis of public companies, combining data, machine learning, AI, and editorial frameworks to generate investment insights.

“StockStory’s AI-driven technology will strengthen CNBC’s ability to provide data-driven insights with faster, more actionable analysis to help investors make informed investment decisions in real time,” the company said [https://seekingalpha.com/pr/20461290-versant-acquires-ai-driven-financial-insights-platform-to-support-cnbc-s-digital-platform].

As part of the transaction, the company said founder and CEO of StockStory Adam Hejl will join Versant and report to Deep Bagchee, Chief Product and Technology Officer for News.

Versant has been aggressive in its pursuit of deals since it debuted on the Nasdaq earlier this year. So far, it has bought Free TV Networks, a provider of over-the-air digital broadcasts, and INDY Cinema Group, a technology platform for movie theaters.

According to a report from _The New York Times_ in March, Versant is also weighing buying Vox Media's podcast network.
